sources.notes: Same Secret Message task as 2174D under the Div.2 contest entry.
MST exchange solution with tree path query implementation.

annotation.summary: Find the cheapest n-1 edges whose selected graph is not a tree.
annotation.constraints: n,m total 2e5; path maximum and exchange checks must be logarithmic or near-linear.
annotation.core_idea: Use the MST as the cheapest tree baseline. Any valid non-tree selection can be improved to one obtained by adding a small number of non-tree edges and deleting heavy eligible MST edges. Evaluate candidates with rooted-tree path/complement maximum queries.

solution_variants.name: mst-exchange-with-path-queries
solution_variants.summary: Kruskal baseline plus non-tree-edge exchange checks on the MST.

tags.evidence: The MST is the baseline and the answer is found through MST exchange cases.
The known implementation performs path/complement maximum queries on the MST.
Range maximum structures over the tree order are needed for fast exchange evaluation.
